title: feed.rs link URL injected unsanitized into HTML before ammonia pass
description:
In feed.rs poll_feed(), a link URL from the feed is interpolated directly into a format string: format\!(r#"

{url}

"#) and then passed to sanitize::sanitize_html(). ammonia allows but does not sanitize the href value for URL injection. A feed whose entry links contain javascript: URLs (e.g. 'javascript:alert(1)') will have that URL both in the href and as the visible text after ammonia processes it, because ammonia 4.x strips unknown protocols only if configured to do so — and the SANITIZE_BUILDER in sanitize.rs only allowlists tag attributes, not URL schemes. The operator-controlled feed is trusted, but a compromised upstream feed will inject XSS into every follower's client that renders the post.
close_reason: Already fixed: feed.rs lines 61-67 filter link URLs to http/https only before interpolation, and line 78 passes through sanitize_html. javascript: URLs are rejected.
